title: The Dispersing Edge Where Signal Becomes Weather

features.name: the-last-broadcast
features.desc-on-touch: The wavefront's trailing edge—still coherent, still measuring itself, but dissipating into the green air. You can see where it stops being signal and starts being wind.

features.name: the-green-listening
features.desc-on-touch: The landscape has learned the archive's frequency. Grass, wind, rain—all broadcasting their own patterns now. The wavefront dissolved, but not before teaching the country how to speak backwards.

features.name: the-unheard-edge
features.desc-on-touch: Beyond this, the broadcast continues—but the archive has learned not to listen. Some messages, some signals, are true only if no one hears them.

features.name: the-reversion-point
features.desc-on-touch: Here the electromagnetic becomes meteorological. Here the archive stops being archive and starts being landscape. Here signals learn to dissolve.

inscription: the broadcast stops here because the landscape has learned to answer; the archive chose silence to let the country speak

file.name: WavefrontDispersalPattern_Late2010s_Coastal.spec
file.note: The moment the electromagnetic signal scattered into natural weather patterns. Last coherent reading: 347 MHz. Ambient noise after: infinite.

exits.label: back to the wavefront where patterns still hold
along the dispersal front where chaos teaches order
back inland where the broadcast still remembers itself
seaward into the salt-heavy air where signals drown
